content.text: Consistency means that multiple agents working on the same task produce outputs that agree with each other — in facts, tone, terminology, and structure. Without deliberate controls, agents running independently will develop contradictions: Agent A defines a term one way, Agent B defines it differently, and the merged result confuses readers. Consistency challenges are distinct from coordination challenges — they are about what agents produce, not how they interact.

attrs.alt: Diagram comparing inconsistent parallel outputs vs consistent outputs with shared context

content.text: Three Sources of Inconsistency
Inconsistency in multi-agent systems comes from three independent sources. Each requires a different fix:
Factual inconsistency: agents state contradictory facts because they retrieved different sources or hallucinated independently
Tonal inconsistency: agents use different writing styles, formality levels, or vocabulary because their system prompts differ
Structural inconsistency: agents format their outputs differently (markdown vs plain text, different heading levels, different list styles)
Fix 1 — Shared Context Injection

The most effective fix for all three sources is injecting a shared context block into every agent's prompt: a glossary of key terms with approved definitions, a style guide excerpt, and a summary of what prior agents have already established. Agents that see the same context produce outputs that naturally align.

from dataclasses import dataclass
from anthropic import AsyncAnthropic

@dataclass
class SharedContext:
    glossary: dict[str, str]      # term → approved definition
    style_guide: str              # 2–3 sentence style summary
    prior_facts: list[str]        # facts established by earlier agents

    def to_prompt_block(self) -> str:
        glossary_lines = "\n".join(
            f"- {term}: {defn}" for term, defn in self.glossary.items()
        )
        facts_lines = "\n".join(f"- {f}" for f in self.prior_facts)
        return (
            f"## Shared Context\n\n"
            f"### Glossary (use these exact definitions)\n{glossary_lines}\n\n"
            f"### Style Guide\n{self.style_guide}\n\n"
            f"### Established Facts\n{facts_lines}"
        )

async def consistent_agent(
    task: str,
    ctx: SharedContext,
    agent_system: str,
    client: AsyncAnthropic,
) -> str:
    """Run an agent with shared context injected into its system prompt."""
    full_system = f"{agent_system}\n\n{ctx.to_prompt_block()}"
    response = await client.messages.create(
        model="claude-sonnet-5",
        max_tokens=2048,
        system=full_system,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": task}],
    )
    return response.content[0].text

# All agents share the same ctx object — same facts, same style guide
ctx = SharedContext(
    glossary={
        "LangGraph": "A Python library for building stateful multi-agent workflows as directed graphs",
        "node": "A Python function that reads from and writes to the LangGraph state",
        "edge": "A directed connection between two nodes in a LangGraph graph",
    },
    style_guide="Use formal technical prose. Define terms on first use. Prefer active voice.",
    prior_facts=[],  # populated as agents complete their sections
)
Fix 2 — Reviewer Agent

A reviewer agent reads all agent outputs together and flags inconsistencies before the results are merged. It does not rewrite the content — it returns a structured list of contradiction locations so the writer can fix them. This is especially effective when the glossary approach is impractical because the task space is too large to pre-define all terms.

from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import Optional
from anthropic import AsyncAnthropic
import json

class Inconsistency(BaseModel):
    location: str        # which agent / section
    issue: str           # description of the contradiction
    suggestion: str      # how to resolve it

class ReviewResult(BaseModel):
    inconsistencies: list[Inconsistency]
    overall_consistent: bool

async def consistency_reviewer(
    outputs: dict[str, str],
    client: AsyncAnthropic,
) -> ReviewResult:
    """Check all agent outputs for factual and tonal inconsistencies."""
    combined = "\n\n".join(
        f"=== {agent} ===\n{text}" for agent, text in outputs.items()
    )
    response = await client.messages.create(
        model="claude-sonnet-5",
        max_tokens=1024,
        system=(
            "You are a consistency reviewer. Read these outputs from multiple agents "
            "and identify any factual contradictions, tonal mismatches, or structural "
            "inconsistencies. Reply with JSON matching this schema: "
            "{inconsistencies: [{location, issue, suggestion}], overall_consistent: bool}"
        ),
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": combined}],
    )
    data = json.loads(response.content[0].text)
    return ReviewResult(**data)

async def merge_with_review(
    outputs: dict[str, str],
    client: AsyncAnthropic,
) -> str:
    review = await consistency_reviewer(outputs, client)
    if review.overall_consistent:
        return "\n\n".join(outputs.values())

    # Feed inconsistencies back to a fixer agent
    issues = "\n".join(
        f"- [{i.location}] {i.issue} → {i.suggestion}"
        for i in review.inconsistencies
    )
    combined = "\n\n".join(outputs.values())
    response = await client.messages.create(
        model="claude-sonnet-5",
        max_tokens=4096,
        system="Rewrite the combined text to resolve all listed inconsistencies.",
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"Text:\n{combined}\n\nIssues:\n{issues}"}],
    )
    return response.content[0].text
Fix 3 — Sequential Agent Access to Prior Output

When parallel execution is not required, the simplest consistency fix is sequential execution: each agent receives the outputs of all prior agents as additional context. This prevents contradictions because each agent can see what has already been established and align its output accordingly.

import asyncio
from anthropic import AsyncAnthropic

async def sequential_consistent_pipeline(
    query: str,
    client: AsyncAnthropic,
) -> list[str]:
    """Each agent gets all prior outputs — natural consistency via context."""
    agents = [
        ("Researcher",  "Retrieve key facts for this topic."),
        ("Analyst",     "Analyze the facts provided. Do not contradict prior outputs."),
        ("Writer",      "Write a concise section. Match the tone and facts in prior outputs."),
    ]
    outputs = []

    for name, system_prompt in agents:
        # Build context from all previous outputs
        prior_context = ""
        if outputs:
            prior_context = "\n\nPrior agent outputs (align with these):\n" + "\n\n".join(
                f"[{a}]: {o}" for a, o in zip([n for n, _ in agents[:len(outputs)]], outputs)
            )

        response = await client.messages.create(
            model="claude-sonnet-5",
            max_tokens=2048,
            system=system_prompt + prior_context,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": query}],
        )
        outputs.append(response.content[0].text)

    return outputs
Consistency vs Diversity

Consistency controls are not always desirable. In evaluation pipelines, you want agents to form independent opinions — injecting shared context would bias them toward agreement, defeating the purpose. The rule: use consistency controls when agents are contributing to a single unified output; avoid them when agents should provide independent perspectives.
Pipeline Type
Use Consistency Controls?
Why
Report writing (parallel sections)
Yes
Sections must read as one coherent document
Fact-checking (multiple reviewers)
No
Reviewers should find different errors independently
Translation (multiple translators)
No
Different translators should be compared, not aligned
Code generation (multiple implementations)
No
Diversity of approaches is the goal
Q&A with multiple specialists
Yes
Final answer must not contradict itself
Debate / adversarial critique
No
Disagreement is the feature, not a bug
Detecting Inconsistency Automatically

Manual review does not scale. Use an automated consistency check that runs after every parallel fan-out: embed each agent output with a lightweight model, compute cosine similarity between key claims, and flag pairs that are semantically contradictory. This catches factual inconsistencies that a simple text comparison would miss.

import asyncio
from anthropic import AsyncAnthropic

async def extract_claims(text: str, client: AsyncAnthropic) -> list[str]:
    """Extract a list of atomic factual claims from an agent output."""
    response = await client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=512,
        system=(
            "Extract all factual claims from the text as a JSON list of strings. "
            "Each claim should be one sentence. Reply with only the JSON array."
        ),
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": text}],
    )
    import json
    return json.loads(response.content[0].text)

async def find_contradictions(
    outputs: dict[str, str],
    client: AsyncAnthropic,
) -> list[tuple[str, str, str]]:
    """Find pairs of contradicting claims across agent outputs.
    Returns list of (agent_a, agent_b, contradiction_description)."""
    all_claims = {}
    for agent, text in outputs.items():
        all_claims[agent] = await extract_claims(text, client)

    contradictions = []
    agents = list(all_claims.keys())
    for i in range(len(agents)):
        for j in range(i + 1, len(agents)):
            a, b = agents[i], agents[j]
            claims_a = "\n".join(all_claims[a])
            claims_b = "\n".join(all_claims[b])
            response = await client.messages.create(
                model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
                max_tokens=256,
                system=(
                    "Identify any factual contradictions between these two claim sets. "
                    "Reply with a JSON object: {has_contradiction: bool, description: str}"
                ),
                messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"Set A:\n{claims_a}\n\nSet B:\n{claims_b}"}],
            )
            import json
            result = json.loads(response.content[0].text)
            if result["has_contradiction"]:
                contradictions.append((a, b, result["description"]))
    return contradictions
Consistency Across Multi-Turn Sessions

In a multi-turn conversation, agents may revisit a topic and contradict what was said earlier. A consistency ledger — a structured list of established facts stored in session state — prevents this. Before each agent run, the orchestrator injects the ledger, and after each run, a lightweight extractor appends new facts to it.

from anthropic import AsyncAnthropic
import json

class ConsistencyLedger:
    """Tracks established facts across multi-turn agent sessions."""
    def __init__(self):
        self.facts: list[str] = []

    def to_context(self) -> str:
        if not self.facts:
            return ""
        lines = "\n".join(f"- {f}" for f in self.facts)
        return f"\n\nEstablished facts (do not contradict these):\n{lines}"

    async def update_from_output(self, output: str, client: AsyncAnthropic) -> None:
        """Extract new facts from an agent output and add to ledger."""
        response = await client.messages.create(
            model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
            max_tokens=256,
            system="Extract new factual claims not already in the list. Reply with a JSON array of strings.",
            messages=[{
                "role": "user",
                "content": f"Existing facts:\n{json.dumps(self.facts)}\n\nNew output:\n{output}",
            }],
        )
        new_facts = json.loads(response.content[0].text)
        self.facts.extend(new_facts)

async def multi_turn_pipeline(
    queries: list[str],
    client: AsyncAnthropic,
) -> list[str]:
    """Run multiple agent turns, maintaining consistency via ledger."""
    ledger = ConsistencyLedger()
    outputs = []

    for query in queries:
        system = "Answer the user query accurately." + ledger.to_context()
        response = await client.messages.create(
            model="claude-sonnet-5",
            max_tokens=1024,
            system=system,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": query}],
        )
        output = response.content[0].text
        outputs.append(output)
        await ledger.update_from_output(output, client)

    return outputs
Testing Consistency in Your Pipeline

Write a consistency regression test that runs your pipeline on a fixed input and checks that key facts appear consistently across all agent outputs. A fact that should appear in every section (the product name, a version number, a defined term) can be asserted with a simple string match.

import pytest
import asyncio
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock

EXPECTED_DEFINITION = "stateful multi-agent workflow library"

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_all_agents_use_same_definition():
    """Assert that no agent contradicts the approved LangGraph definition."""
    mock_client = AsyncMock()

    # Simulate three agents all using the approved definition
    responses = [
        f"LangGraph is a {EXPECTED_DEFINITION} for Python.",
        f"As a {EXPECTED_DEFINITION}, LangGraph uses directed graphs.",
        f"The {EXPECTED_DEFINITION} known as LangGraph provides checkpointing.",
    ]

    call_count = 0
    async def mock_create(**kwargs):
        nonlocal call_count
        resp = MagicMock()
        resp.content = [MagicMock(text=responses[call_count % len(responses)])]
        call_count += 1
        return resp

    mock_client.messages.create = mock_create

    # Run the pipeline
    from mymodule import sequential_consistent_pipeline
    outputs = await sequential_consistent_pipeline("Explain LangGraph", mock_client)

    # Assert the approved definition appears in every output
    for i, output in enumerate(outputs):
        assert EXPECTED_DEFINITION in output, (
            f"Agent {i} output does not contain the approved definition.\n"
            f"Output: {output[:200]}"
        )

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_no_contradictory_version_numbers():
    """Assert no agent mentions a different version than established."""
    # In a real test: run the pipeline, extract version mentions,
    # assert they are all identical.
    pass
Pre-Deployment Consistency Checklist

Before deploying a multi-agent pipeline that produces a merged document, run through this checklist to catch the most common consistency issues. Each item corresponds to a failure mode that is cheap to check but expensive to fix after users encounter it.
Shared context: do all agents receive the same glossary, style guide, and prior-fact list?
Reviewer agent: is there a consistency check step before the final merge?
Golden test: does the pipeline pass a consistency regression test with 20+ representative queries?
Term inventory: have you extracted all technical terms used across agents and confirmed their definitions align?
Tone audit: have you sampled outputs from each agent independently and confirmed the reading level and formality match?
Sequential vs parallel: for any parallel section, is shared context injection active?
Multi-turn: does the pipeline maintain a consistency ledger that updates after each agent turn?
Contradiction detection: does your CI run the automated claim-contradiction check on a golden dataset?
Summary
Consistency means agents agree on facts, tone, and structure — a distinct challenge from coordination
Three sources: factual contradictions, tonal mismatches, and structural divergence
Fix with shared context injection: inject a glossary, style guide, and prior facts into every agent's system prompt
A reviewer agent reads all outputs together and flags contradictions for resolution
Sequential pipelines naturally enforce consistency by giving each agent visibility into prior outputs
Avoid consistency controls in evaluation and adversarial pipelines — independence is the goal there
Automated claim extraction + contradiction detection scales consistency checking to large fan-outs
